marketState: neutral — extreme near-term weather demand offsets bearish seasonality and storage surplus
summary: Front-month NG at $3.279 sits modestly above current fair value ($2.960 midpoint, $3.460 upper band), placing price in a mildly rich but not extreme zone. Critically, both the 1-7 day (pctVs5y 58.6%, aboveMax=true) and 8-16 day (pctVs5y 33.3%, aboveMax=true) demand windows are above the 5-year maximum — a rare dual-window heat configuration that provides real support for the next two weeks. However, demand is already trending lower (change24h -16.7 units) and injection-season storage sits 151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise (+76 Bcf vs +70 forecast). Four-week seasonality is sharply negative (only 19% probability of a higher price, typical move -1.8%, reliability graded LOW), while COT managed-money net at the 18th 5-year percentile (-82,722 contracts) signals a historically crowded short that elevates short-squeeze risk and lowers conviction on the bear side.

longTerm.rationale: Bearish seasonality (19% probability of higher price in 4 weeks, typical move -1.8%, reliability graded LOW with 51-pp band) and a 151 Bcf storage surplus resist longs at current levels, while the winter strip (Jan 2027 at $4.43, +$1.15 vs front) and COT crowded shorts at the 18th 5-year percentile resist outright shorts. September is historically the strongest seasonal month (+3.1%), and if the current dual-window above-max demand conditions persist into August the market could front-run that strength. The conflicting evidence reduces directional conviction to low, warranting patience.
longTerm.invalidation: A decisive multi-session close below $3.00 (fair-value midpoint) would confirm storage and seasonality bears are in control and open a path toward $2.80. A sustained weekly close above $3.46 (upper fair-value band) on robust demand data would flip the posture to long.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for price to pull back to the $2.95–$3.05 fair-value zone on weakening demand before initiating a long targeting the September seasonal and winter premium. Alternatively, a COT managed-money net flip above -50k paired with a bullish storage surprise and price reclaiming $3.35+ would trigger a medium-conviction long.

shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1-7 day (pctVs5y 58.6%, aboveMax=true, rangePos 1.95) and 8-16 day (pctVs5y 33.3%, aboveMax=true, rangePos 1.61) demand windows are above the 5-year maximum, providing exceptional power-burn support across the full near-term weather window. At $3.279, price has roughly $0.18 of headroom to the upper fair-value band at $3.460 — a reachable target if heat holds. The 5m/15m RSI near 34 signals near-oversold conditions on the intraday pullback, while the 4-hour timeframe is bullish (RSI 50.3, positive MACD, 50>200 EMA), suggesting the near-term path of least resistance is a bounce toward $3.40–$3.46.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close below $3.17 — meaningfully below the Sep contract price of $3.226 — would signal that heat bulls have lost control of the tape, likely triggered by a sudden weather pattern change, a demand collapse larger than the current 24-hour trend, or a storage build significantly above expectations.

Weather demand 8-16D also above 5-year maximum (pctVs5y 33.3%, rangePos 1.61, aboveMax=true) — dual-window heat event sustains demand into second week

COT managed-money net at 18th 5-year percentile (-82,722 contracts, heavily short) — crowded position elevates short-squeeze risk on bullish catalysts
Seasonality: only 19% probability of higher price in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.8%, reliability graded LOW (51-pp spread)
risks: Demand trend already falling sharply (-16.7 units in 24 hours); if heat breaks faster than expected the near-term long loses its primary catalyst and price could revert to fair-value midpoint near $2.96
